measurement_caveat: WebSearch proxy is DIRECTIONAL ONLY; it cannot observe true ChatGPT/Claude/Perplexity citations. Domain is 8 days post-rebrand (sayfeai.com); near-zero indexation expected. 0/N is not a regression signal.
sample_note: 6 WebSearch-proxy searches across 6 priority-bucket query_ids. Sampled chatgpt-business-vs-plus first so today's diagnosis could directly verify the queued data-privacy/admin-enforcement open-rec from 2026-05-25. Compliance/assessment/partner buckets covered with 1-3 reps each.

results.notes: Comparison-table pages own this query. CITED MECHANISM (consensus across tactiq.io + aibusinessweekly.net + intuitionlabs.ai + openai enterprise-privacy): 'On Plus, OpenAI may use conversations for model training by default; each user opts out individually via Settings -> Data Controls, but the opt-out only covers that one account and doesn't extend to the team. On Business, data exclusion is organizational and contractual -- not an opt-out toggle but a structural guarantee of the plan.' Our page had data-privacy ticked in the comparison table but did NOT articulate this per-user-opt-out-vs-contractual-org mechanism in either the visible FAQ or the FAQPage JSON-LD. Pricing consensus (April 2 2026 cut): Plus $20/mo, Business $25/mo monthly OR $20/mo annual, 2-seat min. Already on page. -> diagnosis directly clears yesterday's queued open-rec.

SB 26-189 (ADMT) signed by Polis 2026-05-12 (this run's date) per consumerfinancemonitor.com; effective 2027-01-01. Repeals & replaces SB 24-205. Small-business carve-out: 40-or-fewer-employee firms generally NOT 'deployers' (but hiring/compensation ADMT use can still pull them in). AG has stated no enforcement until rulemaking concludes. Sayfeai's Colorado/EU blog carries the alias (shipped 2026-05-22) but still 0% citation -- expected on an 8-day-old domain. Pillar handoff (/compliance/colorado-ai-act) remains outstanding for Claude Code. Note: prior ledger logged May 14 sign date based on different source set; today's consensus is May 12 sign / May 14 legislative passage announcement. Both within the same week.

ispartnersllc.com still owns the '60 seconds / no signup' promise. trycomplianceiq.com back at #4 (was off the list 2026-05-25). NEW competitor: clearact.net ('Find out your risk level in 2 minutes; 15 questions; instant EU AI Act risk classification; no signup'). AIComply also no-signup-required for 3 quick questions. Pillar handoff (/compliance-assessment) STILL the highest-leverage Engine-1 conversion page; build it with H1 + lead promising 5-minute / no signup / no card / instant risk classification.

Article 50 transparency obligations effective Aug 2 2026 (chatbot-disclosure + machine-readable AI-generated content marking). Latham & Watkins flag the EU Digital Omnibus deadline-extension thread (high-risk Annex III may slip to Dec 2 2027). SME simplified compliance pathways + R&D exemptions exist. Pillar handoff (/compliance/eu-ai-act) outstanding.

Engine 2. AI governance still framed as 'high-margin frontier' for MSPs in 2026 (ManagedServicesJournal). Compass MSP + Guardz position to SMBs specifically. 94% of SMBs now use an MSP (LogMeIn). Sayfeai has zero MSP-reseller offering -> strategy proposal only, not a fabricable page edit. No new competitor crossed the 3+ threshold.

Business: $20/user annual or $25 monthly, 2-seat min, post April-2-2026 cut. Enterprise: custom, typical $60-$100+/seat at 150+ seat floor. 'Genuine triggers for Enterprise over Business' cited consistently: 150+ users, regulated industries (HIPAA, EKM, data residency), unlimited usage, SCIM + advanced SSO. Our /blog/chatgpt-business-vs-enterprise-2026 page is not surfacing -- but no high-confidence non-churn patch this run (the Business pricing/seat math is already accurate on the page per 2026-05-23 work).
